parsed_review.summary: CP00-269 is a descriptor-only closeout pack covering RP07.P09.M05.S09-S18 (risk A, 10 units: the "Permission And Audit Binding" middle rows, 9 implementation + 1 claude_review). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is supported. The registry binding, closeout-pack-plan.json, next-pack-queue.json (live latest CP00-268 -> next CP00-269), and weighted-implementation-ledger all agree on the same 10 units, titles, deliverable types, risk class, range, upstream (CP00-268), and next pack (CP00-270 / RP07.P09.M05.S19). service.js builds every row as descriptor_only with runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false; validators.js enforces coverage/descriptor/row/authority drift and its freeze wrapper does not mask failures; the contract projection (current_pack, review_binding_mid_descriptor, cp269_requirements, cp269_no_write_attestation, historical_pack_bindings tail, root authority_boundary) matches the source descriptor and is deep-equality checked by the validator script. All 7 public exports are defined and re-exported via the index.js wildcard, the README has a CP00-269 entry, and model.test.js covers coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, evidence packets, and handoff. Authority boundaries hold throughout: claude_is_final_approval=false, read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true, and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes is used only for the deterministic evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only...no_write), not product runtime. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are attested and the manifest correctly declares production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review with no premature production-ready=true claim. CP178+ hardening holds: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1469-byte valid JSON, end_turn), forbidden_tools_present=false, review schema present, and the descriptor/contract invalid_review_blockers enumerate the required rejection cases (failed authentication, zero-byte output, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields). Command evidence shows all 19 build/test/validate commands exiting 0 and Hermes gate H07 passed; the three post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this read_only_claude_run step. One non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json redundantly classifies new-session-handoff.md and the cp00-269/ directory under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, but this is cosmetic (active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty and the truly-unrelated docx/ldip files are correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs). No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack and goal closeout are not blocked. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver; human final approval remains required for any enterprise trust claim.

findings.title: Redundant file classification in changed-file-scope.json (cosmetic, non-blocking)
findings.details: new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-269/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). The two arrays use different definitions of 'preserved' than the manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, which lists only the truly-unrelated docx and ldip files. This is functionally harmless: active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (nothing required was dropped), the cp00-269/ directory legitimately holds this pack's own evidence, and the genuinely unrelated files (the .docx and c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs. No pack-integrity impact.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the scope-generator so a file is listed in exactly one bucket (pack-related untracked vs. unrelated-preserved) to match the manifest's narrower definition. No change required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_informational; no remediation required before pack or goal closeout
